title: regelia regnellii
intro: Regelia regnellii, also known as Emu Bush or Stinkweed, is a native Australian shrub that belongs to the Myoporum family. It has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from congestion. Herbal preparations of Regelia regnellii can be made into teas, tinctures, and topical creams, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
